text:
Rikhav Securities Limited Reports EBITDA of ₹32.53 Cr & PAT of ₹18.96 Cr in FY26

Rikhav Securities Limited, one of the leading diversified stock market services providers has announced its Audited Financial Results for H2 & FY26.

H2 FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1604.95 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 6.57 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 1.20 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 0.31

FY26 Financial Highlights
* Total Income of ₹ 1991.62 Cr
* EBITDA of ₹ 32.53 Cr
* PAT of ₹ 18.96 Cr
* EPS of ₹ 4.95

Financial & Business Updates FY26
- Invested ₹5.40 Cr in technology infrastructure and software during FY26, leading to higher depreciation
- Profitability impacted by ₹22.93 Cr loss from share trading activities
- Unrealised SLBM profit of ₹4.33 Cr not recognised in FY26 financials and expected to be realised in upcoming quarter
- Current liabilities increased mainly due to SLBM borrowings of ₹47.34 Cr

Currently, the stock is trading at ₹47.40, with a Mcap of 181.50 Cr. (As on 23rd May 2026)

text:
TRANSRAIL LIGHTING APPROVES ₹203 CR CAPEX PLAN ⚡🏗️

• 💰 Company approved capex plan worth approximately ₹203 crore

• 🏭 Capex aimed at debottlenecking, machine upgradation & meeting rising order book requirements

• ⚙️ Tower segment to receive ₹19.41 crore investment over next 12–15 months

• 🔌 Conductor segment to get ₹9 crore investment for capacity enhancement & operational upgrades

• 🛠️ Major allocation of ₹169.04 crore towards tools and plants for construction activities

• 📦 Additional ₹6 crore earmarked under other capex requirements

• 📈 Existing tower and conductor capacities currently operating at 100% utilisation

• 🚀 Planned upgrades expected to increase production capacity by 6,000 MT per annum along with machine modernization

• 🏗️ Company had already expanded tower manufacturing capacity to 192,000 MT PA and conductor capacity to 49,200 KM PA under earlier approved capex

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strong capex commitment backed by full utilisation levels and healthy order book supports future growth visibility)

text:
US NAVY RESUMES ESCORTS IN STRAIT OF HORMUZ

U.S. Navy has restarted assisting vessel crossings through the Strait of Hormuz, guiding ships under a renewed “Project Freedom” operation.

A Greek supertanker carrying 2 million barrels of crude was escorted through the waterway toward India after being stuck in the region since March.

Officials said the Navy plans to help around a dozen additional tankers and container ships in the coming days

text:
NLC INDIA SIGNS MoU WITH IIT(ISM) TEXMIN FOR CRITICAL MINERALS EXPLORATION ⛏️🔋

• 🤝 NLC India signed MoU with IIT(ISM) TEXMIN, Dhanbad for critical & strategic mineral exploration and research

• 🌍 Partnership aligned with India’s National Critical Mineral Mission and mineral security objectives

• 🪨 NLC India actively pursuing exploration & utilisation of critical minerals from primary and secondary resources

• 🏆 Company has already secured two Critical Mineral Blocks through transparent auction process

• 🔬 Collaboration to focus on critical mineral exploration, beneficiation & extraction technologies

• ♻️ NLC India studying recovery of rare earth elements (REE) and other trace minerals from overburden, lignite/coal & mine tailings

• 🧠 TEXMIN to support advanced mining research, technology development and knowledge sharing

• ⚡ Partnership aimed at strengthening indigenous critical mineral value chains for clean energy & strategic industries

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens NLC India’s long-term positioning in strategic minerals and supports diversification into future-focused clean energy supply chains)

Top fund managers have very different takes on Indian IT.

Rajeev Thakkar is finding value at current levels and is buying IT across funds.

Prashant Jain is neutral. His view the sector may need 2 more years for better clarity.

Kenneth Andrade has completely exited IT, citing uncertainty around the real impact of AI on the sector.

S. Naren, the contrarian, is also seeing value and continues to hold IT stocks.

Samir Arora is bearish on traditional IT, with zero allocation there, but prefers select tech and capital market plays.

Sunil Singhania has no exposure to traditional IT stocks, but is buying a product-based tech company.

It will be interesting to see who gets this right.

text:
JK TYRE APPROVES ₹4,980 CR TYRE CAPACITY EXPANSION 🚛🛞

• 🏭 Board approved phased expansion of Truck & Bus Radial (TBR) and Passenger Car Radial (PCR) tyre capacities 

• 📍 Expansion planned at Chennai Tyre Plant (CTP) and Vikrant Tyre Plant (VTP) 

• ⚙️ Existing TBR/PCR capacity stands at 210 lakh tyres per annum (including under implementation capacity) 

• 📈 Company plans to increase TBR/PCR capacity by 24% 

• 💰 Total proposed investment pegged at around ₹4,980 Cr in phases 

• 🏦 Expansion to be funded through internal accruals and debt 

• ⏳ Proposed capacity addition targeted by FY30 

• 🔥 Current utilisation already above 90% of installed capacity 

• 🚚 Company cited strong demand momentum across tyre categories as key reason for expansion 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large capex signals strong demand outlook, supports market share gains and strengthens long-term growth visibility)

text:
PONDY OXIDES TO SET UP ₹200 CR COPPER RECYCLING PLANT ♻️⚡

• 🏭 Board approved new Copper Recycling Plant at Thervoykandigai, Tamil Nadu 

• 🔩 Planned capacity of 36,000 MTPA LME Grade A Copper Cathode in two phases 

• ⚙️ Plant to use integrated Pyro-refining & Electro-refining technology 

• 💰 Total project cost estimated at around ₹200 Cr, funded via internal accruals 

• 🌍 Target markets include both domestic demand and exports 

• ⏳ Commissioning targeted on or before December 2026 

• 🔗 Project aimed at forward integration into value-added non-ferrous recycling business 

• 📈 Company expects stronger margins, improved product mix and operational synergies post commissioning 

• 🌱 Plant also expected to reduce carbon footprint and strengthen sustainable manufacturing positioning 

• 🇮🇳 Initiative to help reduce India’s copper import dependency 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Large value-added expansion strengthens copper recycling presence, improves long-term growth visibility and supports margin expansion)

Indian Railways’ ₹40,000Cr Wagon Tender Signals Multi-Year Demand Visibility

Indian Railways plans ₹40,000Cr freight wagon procurement over 3–4 years.

Tender size targets nearly 100,000 wagons, exceeding the 2022 procurement cycle.

Annual procurement could reach 35,000–40,000 wagons under phased ordering structure.

First tranche of orders likely during July–September quarter this fiscal year.

Initiative aims to absorb expanded manufacturing capacities across domestic wagon makers.

Previous 2022 tender worth ₹32,000Cr also covered roughly 100,000 wagons.

🗞 BEAT THE STREET BULLETIN 🗞
Date: 26 May 2026
Telegram: https://t.me/BeatTheStreetnews

📍 ORDER WIN

L&T: Wins multiple “Significant” orders worth ₹1,000–2,500 Cr including JSW Utkal Steel piling project, IWAI EPC contracts and Mumbai yacht marina project.

BHARAT ELECTRONICS (BEL): Secures additional defence and electronics orders worth ₹608 Cr across communication systems, radars, avionics and simulators.

ACME SOLAR: Signs 300 MW / 1,200 MWh FDRE PPA with SECI at ₹6.28/unit for 25 years.

MARINE ELECTRICALS: Bags ₹96.4 Cr order from Princeton Digital Group for power distribution systems.

GPT INFRAPROJECTS: Wins ₹72 Cr Eastern Railway contract for supply of PSC sleepers.

BALU FORGE: Secures maiden aerospace components order from US-based Alpha Aircraft Systems.

KELLTON TECH: Wins UAE digital transformation deal to build unified enterprise platform.

APTECH: Bags ₹9.3 Cr government contract for computer-based examination services.

KD GREEN INDUSTRIES: Subsidiary Shivam Pipes wins ₹9.06 Cr RDSS order from MePDCL.

DESCO INFRATECH: Receives ₹3.15 Cr order for remediation, cable laying and infrastructure work.

📍 ACQUISITION

AXISCADES: Sells heavy engineering, automotive and energy business to Akkodis for $30.63 Mn (~₹261 Cr).

📍 COLLABORATION

ACME SOLAR: Signs long-term PPA with SECI for assured peak renewable power supply.

📍 NEW PROJECTS

ADANI GREEN ENERGY: Operationalises 1,990 MWh BESS and 50 MW solar project at Khavda, Gujarat.

PACE DIGITEK: Undertaking major BESS expansion with additional 2.5 GWh and new 5 GWh facility.

SKM EGG PRODUCTS: Announces ₹400 Cr capex to expand poultry capacity and bio-gas operations.

REGAAL RESOURCES: Doubles crushing capacity to 1,650 TPD and launches glucose & maltodextrin facilities.

ORCHID PHARMA: Announces ₹750 Cr capex for antibiotics integration and expansion projects.

📍 FUNDRAISING

STEEL EXCHANGE INDIA: Raises ₹85 Cr via warrants and approves ₹350 Cr preferential issue for growth and debt reduction.

📍 FII STAKE CHANGES / BOUGHT-SOLD

PREMIER ENERGIES: Promoter family sells 5.29% stake; strong buying seen from global funds, MFs and insurers.

📍 INCORPORATE / CORPORATE STRUCTURE

STUDDS ACCESSORIES: Incorporates wholly-owned subsidiary SMK Helmets Europe SRL in Italy.

📍 IT NEWS

TATA ELXSI: Launches AI-powered “ViTel” material intelligence platform for med-tech industry.

MOBIKWIK: Receives RBI in-principle approval for Payment Aggregator-Physical license.

EKI ENERGY SERVICES: Gets 25-year inter-state electricity trading license from CERC.

📍 LAUNCHES

REGAAL RESOURCES: Launches liquid glucose and maltodextrin manufacturing facilities under maize segment.

📍 OTHERS

HITECH CORPORATION: Promoter group announces voluntary delisting plan at indicative price of ₹353/share.

USHANTI COLOUR CHEM: Approves migration from NSE SME platform to NSE mainboard.

H.G. INFRA ENGINEERING: Declared qualified bidder for UP transmission project under BOOT model.

BRITANNIA INDUSTRIES: Plans price hikes in Good Day biscuit portfolio amid rising input costs.

INDIAN MARITIME SECTOR: Government pushes reforms, digitalisation and AI adoption for sector growth.

FERTILIZER SECTOR: Government likely to increase subsidies amid rising input and gas prices.

INSURANCE SECTOR: IRDAI proposes linking KMP pay with policyholder outcomes and performance.

SOLAR SECTOR: MNRE may allow deadline extensions for delayed solar projects.

TAIWAN MARKET: Taiwan overtakes India as 5th largest stock market driven by AI and semiconductor rally.

text:
SUN PHARMA TO PRESENT LONG-TERM UNLOXCYT DATA AT ASCO 2026 🧬🩺

• 🧪 Updated pivotal trial data for UNLOXCYT™ (cosibelimab-ipdl) in locally advanced cutaneous squamous cell carcinoma (laCSCC) to be presented at ASCO 2026 

• 📈 Objective response rate stood at 50%, including 27% complete responses & 23% partial responses 

• ⏳ Median duration of response not yet reached after 31 months follow-up, highlighting durable efficacy 

• 👨‍⚕️ Study involved 64 laCSCC patients; median treatment duration was 60 weeks 

• ✅ Only 1 patient experienced Grade ≥3 immune-related adverse event, indicating manageable safety profile 

• 🌍 Management highlighted this as the second largest prospective study of laCSCC patients treated with PD-(L)1 monotherapy 

• 💊 UNLOXCYT is approved for metastatic and locally advanced CSCC patients not eligible for curative surgery/radiation 

🟢 Impact: Positive (Strengthens Sun Pharma’s specialty oncology portfolio with strong long-term efficacy and safety data for innovative skin cancer therapy)

Captain Polyplast Limited - Q4 & FY26 Concall Highlights

Company Overview
Captain Polyplast Limited has been a leading player in the Micro Irrigation Systems (MIS) industry for nearly three decades. The Company has strategically diversified into the Solar EPC segment, focusing on solar water pumping systems and rooftop solar solutions. Backed by a strong distribution network of over 750 dealers across key agricultural states, the Company provides integrated solutions along with robust after-sales support to farmers and customers.

Financial Performance

Q4 FY26 Performance
* Reported its highest-ever quarterly revenue of ₹142 Cr, registering an 80% YoY growth from ₹79.1 Cr in Q4 FY25.
* EBITDA increased 66% YoY to ₹14.16 Cr.
* EBITDA Margin stood at 9.96%.
* Net Profit surged 91% YoY to ₹9.76 Cr, while EPS stood at ₹1.64.

FY26 Performance
* Total Income reached ₹419 Cr, reflecting a robust 44% YoY growth over FY25.
* Full-year EBITDA stood at ₹46.32 Cr with an EBITDA Margin of 11.03%.

Revenue Mix & Profitability
Business Segment Mix
* Current revenue mix stands at approximately 80:20 / 85:15 (MIS : Solar EPC).
* Management aims to achieve a 50:50 revenue mix within the next two years.

Growing Non-Subsidy Contribution
* The cash (non-subsidy) segment contributed 15% of MIS revenues during FY26.
* The rooftop solar business operates entirely on a cash-and-carry model.

Profitability Outlook
Management highlighted that solar pump margins remain structurally aligned with MIS margins, indicating no margin dilution from the evolving business mix.

Operations & Expansion
Ahmedabad Facility & Backward Integration
* The new 70,000 sq. ft. Ahmedabad manufacturing facility is now operational.
* The facility manufactures high-margin micro irrigation components in-house.
* Manufacturing Capacity. Combined capacity across Rajkot, Kurnool, and Ahmedabad plants can support up to ₹400 Cr MIS revenue without any major additional capex.

Solar Pumps Business Momentum
* Executed 1,500 solar pumps in Maharashtra during the first year of operations.
* Secured a fresh 500-pump order worth ₹11 Cr.

Raw Material & Margin Trends
Q4 profitability was impacted by a sharp increase in raw material prices during March 2026, primarily due to unforeseen geopolitical developments. This remained the key reason behind the 86 bps margin contraction.

Management Guidance & Growth Outlook
* Management is targeting 20–25% CAGR growth in the MIS segment over the next three years, primarily driven by market share gains.
* The Solar EPC segment is expected to grow at a faster pace to gradually match MIS revenues.

Export Strategy
* Exports currently contribute around 5% of MIS revenue.
* Focus remains on drip irrigation products for African and Latin American markets.
* Management targets double-digit export contribution within the next five years.

🏥 Yatharth Hospital Accelerates Expansion Journey  
Q4 Revenue Surges 47% YoY | Targets ~5,000 Beds Over Next 3 Years | MCap ₹6,718 Cr

📌 Expansion & Capacity Growth

- Total operational bed capacity expanded to 2,555 beds, excluding the under-construction 250-bed Gurugram hospital.

- Company plans to scale capacity to nearly 5,000 beds within the next 3 years, strengthening its presence across North India.

- Newly launched hospitals in Greater Faridabad, Faridabad Sector-20, New Delhi, and Agra contributed ₹753 million, accounting for 22% of Group revenues in Q4 FY26.

- Yatharth acquired a 250-bed under-construction hospital in Gurugram for ₹100 crore, expected to become operational by April 2027.

📌 Financial Performance

- Revenue from Operations grew 47% YoY to ₹3,416 million in Q4 FY26.

- FY26 Revenue from Operations increased 36% YoY to ₹12,072 million.

- Q4 EBITDA stood at ₹799 million, up 37% YoY, with EBITDA Margin at 23.4%.

- FY26 EBITDA came in at ₹2,921 million, registering 30% YoY growth, with EBITDA Margin at 24.2%.

- Excluding new hospitals, the company reported a strong Adjusted EBITDA Margin of 30.4% in Q4 FY26 and 28.5% for FY26.

- Q4 PAT rose 15% YoY to ₹447 million, with PAT Margin at 13.1%.

- FY26 PAT increased 30% YoY to ₹1,703 million, with PAT Margin at 14.1%.

📌 Operational Highlights

- The newly commissioned Agra hospital achieved monthly revenues of nearly ₹7 crore and delivered Q4 EBITDA margin above 15%.

- Successfully performed Agra’s first Da Vinci Robotic Surgeries, marking a major technological milestone.

- Yatharth Hospital has become the exclusive clinical partner to Noida International Airport for Medical Value Travel (MVT) initiatives.

📌 Shareholder Value Creation

- The company has delivered an impressive 188% shareholder return since listing.

- Current Market Capitalisation stands at approximately ₹6,718 crore.

NLC India Signs MoU with NPCIL for Development of Nuclear Power Projects through a JV Company

Indian EV purchases are increasingly being driven by arithmetic, not aspiration.🧮

The Indian EV transition is increasingly being pulled by economics, not only pushed by policy. A cumulative ₹5/litre rise in petrol and diesel in May has made running-cost savings far more tangible for retail customers and fleets.

The illustrative math in the ₹11–12 lakh segment: EVs cost more upfront, but their 5-year running cost is far lower than petrol, diesel, or CNG. That is why EVs are increasingly being treated as a financial hedge, in addition to being a green choice. 

The market is already responding. EV PV retail share rose to 5.77% in April 2026 from 3.70% a year earlier.

EVs may still face friction on model choice, charging convenience, and resale value, to be addressed. 

The implication for industry is significant: product positioning, financing, and resale support will matter as much as battery specs. In a market like India, ownership economics will decide scale.

GPIL 

Approved setting up of a **20 GWh BESS project** and signed long-term agreements with **EVE Power** for Grade-1 **628 Ah LFP cells** and with **Shanghai Shenyi Roche Energy Technology Ltd.** for BESS Balance of System supply, thereby securing the supply chain for the project. The company also announced an investment of **INR 200 crore** for setting up **GPIL BESS**.

Utssav CZ Gold Jewels Reports 136% PAT Growth and PAT Margin Expansion of 123 Bps in FY26

Utssav CZ Gold Jewels Limited one of the leading manufacturers of 18K, 20K, and 22K CZ gold, plain gold, gold & diamond jewellery has announced its unaudited Financial Results for H2 FY26 & FY26.

Key Financial Highlights H2 FY26
- Total Income of ₹ 681.00 Cr, YoY growth of 87.79%
- EBITDA of ₹ 48.67 Cr, YoY growth of 98.95%
- EBITDA Margin of 7.15%, YoY growth of 40 Bps 
- PAT of ₹ 29.64 Cr, YoY growth of 95.20%
- PAT(%) of 4.35%, YoY growth of 17 Bps
- EPS of ₹ 11.86, YoY growth of 82.74%

Key Financial Highlights FY26
- Total Income of ₹ 1,157.46 Cr, YoY growth of 78.71%
- EBITDA of ₹ 93.70 Cr, YoY growth of 132.28%
- EBITDA Margin of 8.10%, YoY growth of 187 Bps
- PAT of ₹ 59.06 Cr, YoY growth of 135.67%
- PAT(%) of 5.10%, YoY growth of 123 Bps
- EPS of ₹ 23.95, YoY growth of 105.93%

During H2 FY26 The Company Received Board approval to establish a wholly owned subsidiary in Dubai, UAE, marking a strategic step toward expanding the Company’s global footprint.

*CRYOGENIC OGS* receives major international recognition with ADNOC vendor approval 🔥

The company has received enlistment approval from ADNOC (Abu Dhabi National Oil Company) for the Metering Skids category, marking a significant milestone in its global expansion journey.

With this approval, Cryogenic OGS is now recognised as an approved vendor for Metering Skids (Liquids & Gases), allowing the company to participate in ADNOC tenders and upcoming business opportunities across the Middle East energy market.

ADNOC is one of the world’s leading oil & gas companies, and this approval strengthens Cryogenic OGS’ credibility in the international oil & gas equipment space.

Positive Takeaways:

• Entry into ADNOC’s approved vendor ecosystem
• Opens doors for high-value international tenders
• Strengthens presence in Middle East oil & gas markets
• Enhances global credibility and business visibility
• Expected to support long-term business development initiatives

A strong development that reflects the company’s gr
CLSA-IN-Sterlite_Technologies_Ltd-20260524-20260524141639381203.pdf

Paramatrix FY26: Cash Flow Jumps 2.5x, Building Strong Growth Foundation

Paramatrix Technologies Limited has reported its FY26 audited performance, reflecting stable revenues, strong cash generation, and continued strategic investments.

* Total Income steady at ₹32.6 Cr (+4% YoY); operational revenue remained largely stable at ₹28.85 Cr
* Operating Cash Flow surged 2.5x to ₹6.9 Cr, driven by improved collections and tighter working capital
* Reported profitability was impacted by a one-time ₹2.74 Cr provision for bad and doubtful debts, masking the underlying business stability.
* Strategic acquisition: 51% stake in Metasys Software to strengthen capabilities and expand market reach
* ₹19.96 Cr invested towards infrastructure development and ₹8.13 Cr towards product development, enhancing operational capacity and strengthening core capabilities to drive scalable growth from FY27 onwards.
* PLAYMITY wins “Best Product Innovation” (BFSI), validating strong product-market fit

These are companies listed on our exchanges with unusually high operating profit margins compared to their sector averages.

That makes them worth studying.

The key is to understand what drives those margins — pricing power, niche products, efficiency, brand strength or superior business mix.

A few may be cyclicals, so ignore them and focus on businesses where high margins are sustainable.

No buy sell suggestion

Anondita Medicare’s H2FY26 numbers show one clear theme: scale + margins improving together.

Revenue jumped from ₹45.59 Cr to ₹83.32 Cr — up 82.77% YoY.

But the stronger part is profitability:

EBITDA: ₹15.93 Cr → ₹31.46 Cr
PAT: ₹10.47 Cr → ₹21.28 Cr
EBITDA Margin: 34.94% → 37.76%
At the same time, the company is preparing for its next leg of growth with the Noida facility expansion.
Key expansion details:
• Approved capacity addition: ~1,360 million pieces p.a.
• Existing capacity: 869 million pieces p.a.
• 16 manufacturing lines under WIP
• Estimated project cost: ~₹75 Cr
• Promoters infused ₹30 Cr via preferential issue at ₹1,022/share

**Key Growth Drivers**

1. Global Expansion Scale exports beyond Africa into high-potential international markets

2. In advanced stages, enabling access to global markets, with exports targeted to begin by end-FY26 or early next Q1FY26

3. Enhance quality, scalability, and margins through advanced manufacturing

4. Launch patented female condoms to address underserved demand

HSBC on Hindalco
Buy, TP Rs 1430

CITI on Hindalco
Neutral, TP Rs 1170

At Rs101.8bn (6% ahead), 4Q consol EBITDA rose 6% yoy/28% qoq

QoQ increase is attributed to better performance across divisions.

India upstream: Rs54.5bn vs. Rs48.3bn in 3Q; India downstream: Rs2.6bn vs. Rs2.3bn; Copper: Rs9bn vs. Rs6bn; Novelis: Rs42bn vs. Rs31bn.

India upstream EBITDA/t was $1,756 vs. $1,572 in 3Q and should continue to rally (spot $3,650), offset partly by 5% higher costs

Est debt should peak at $8.2bn in FY27 & fall thereafter ($6.8bn as of Mar26); estimate net D/EBITDA at 1.6x vs. 1.8x in Mar26
Global team is structurally bullish aluminium; CY27 estimate is $3,800

Maintain Neutral, given LME upside from spot may not be significant

Potential upsides such as (1) Bay Minette profitability, (2) higher India smelting capacity, and (3) captive coal are back-ended.

While it expects new launches, portfolio expansion and acquisitions to lead overall growth, expect the full impact of these initiatives only from FY28

Buy, TP Rs 1700

This was supported by the 160bps QoQ gross margin expansion largely on Pen-G plant ramp-up.

While $2Bn US revenue run-rate (vs $1.6Bn in FY26) depends on Lannett deal closure (2QFY27), 21% margin guidance is 50-100bps higher than street/BofA estimates.

Buy, TP Rs 1550

4Q broadly in line with estimates (+2% vs. GSe), with sales/EBITDA growing by +6%/+1% yoy driven by strong growth in Europe (+29% yoy) and growth markets (+25% yoy) partially offset by a slower US which is still seeing impacts of the gRevlimid ramp-down (-13% yoy).

EBITDA margin came in steady at 20.3% (-16bps vs. GSe), as higher GMs (+216bps yoy) were offset by higher employee costs

Co aspires to achieve high single digit topline growth (ex-gRevlimid) while guiding to maintain margins at 21%+ mark in FY27

JK Lakshmi Cement: HOLD | TP 680 | Leverage to rise amidst weak profitability

Le Travenues Technology: HOLD | TP 180 | Subdued quarter; near-term headwinds persist as hotels remain a long-term catalyst

Hohn’s High-Conviction Alpha Trap

Sir Chris Hohn runs a hyper-concentrated $21B book with roughly $14B parked in a single bet on GE Aerospace, weaponizing a multi-decade moat where zero new engine competitors have entered in 50 years.

Retail loves the "charitable billionaire" narrative, but Hohn’s extreme concentration introduces massive systemic tail risk. A single operational drawdown or aerospace de-rating would completely vaporize the fund's capital.

This regime shift highlights a structural corporate margin play: dominant global duopolies can easily pass through inflation-fueled input costs, squeezing labor stats while capturing immense, unchecked pricing power.

Bottom Line: Forget diversification. True alpha in this era is about finding structural monopolies with bulletproof unit economics that can survive any macro liquidity trap.

**Target Price: ₹3,114 (from ₹2,342)**

• Diversification strategy gaining strong momentum  
• Q4FY26 performance beat estimates  
• Non-auto business traction remains strong  
• ADS division driving global growth  
• FY27 revenue growth seen ~22%  
• FY28 revenue growth seen ~19%  
• FY27 EBITDA margin seen ~19%  
• FY28 EBITDA margin seen ~19.5%  
• FY27 EPS estimates raised ~8%  
• FY28 EPS estimates raised ~11%  
• Valuation rerating potential remains strong  
• Non-auto diversification improving visibility

Nomura on Sansera Engineering

Maintain Buy; Hike TP to Rs 3114 from Rs 2342

Diversification gaining pace

Q4FY26 beats estimates

Strong global traction in non-auto led by ADS division keeps growth visibility high

See revenue growth of 22%/19% YoY in FY27/28 and EBITDA margins of 19%/19.5%
Hike EPS estimates by 8%/11%

Believe Sansera’s valuations can trade at the upper end of its 25-35x historical trading band

See improved longer-term growth visibility driven by diversification into the non-auto segment

Execution at scale to beat inefficient govt. run businesses

Debt - not concerned

Valuations- no longer cheap, but some stocks still in line with peers

Adani Power - effectively a 2-3 player market where equipment are a bottleneck and the company has blocked both Indian suppliers for years to come

Adani Green - no doubts on their strategic advantage with land-connectivity, but remain uncomfortable with the valuations

• Debt levels not seen concerning  
• Bond yields and repayments comfortable  
• Valuations no longer appear inexpensive  
• Some group firms aligned with peers

**Jefferies on Honasa Consumer (Mamaearth)**  

**Target Price: ₹565**  
**Recommendation: Buy**  

• Distribution realignment phase largely over  
• Growth momentum improving steadily  
• Mamaearth delivered mid-teens growth  
• Younger brands continued strong traction  
• EBITDA margins reached record highs  
• High-teens revenue growth guided ahead  
• Annual margin expansion guided at ~100 bps  
• Long-term compounding story emerging  
• Improving execution may attract investors

CLSA on Honasa

O-P, TP Rs 434

reported revenue growth of 23% YoY (28% growth ex-change in accounting) 4%/6% ahead of our estimate/consensus. 

Volume grew 30% while its margin expanded more than 650bps YoY, leading to a beat on Ebitda of 19%/24% versus our estimate/consensus. 

Three things stood out for us. 

Fisrst, Mamaearth grew in the teens YoY and management expects double-digit growth to continue. 

Second, offtake growth in general trade and modern trade was up 30% YoY, a healthy sign of rising brand traction. 

Third, operating leverage, albeit on a soft base, helped Ebitda beat our estimate by more than 140bps. 

We think Honasa’s runway to scale brands via its focus categories and hero SKUs remains long. 

Execution of the Mamaearth turnaround continues to improve and we expect margin expansion to continue.

CLSA on Nykaa

O-P TP Rs 338

reported 28% revenue growth in 4QFY26 and its highest-ever Ebitda margin of 8.4%, up 195bps YoY, with Ebitda 8% above consensus. 

Beauty NSV grew 29% YoY with margin-accretive own brands up 54%, driving a +70bps Ebitda margin. 

Fashion saw 42% YoY NSV growth with Ebitda breakeven. 

Ebit missed our estimate due to higher-than-expected depreciation. 

We lower our FY27-28CL earnings estimates by 1%-2% on account of higher depreciation, but raise our target price from Rs328 to Rs338 to reflect faster sales growth. 

We expect Ebitda margin expansion to continue, driven by rising salience of own brands and operating leverage.

Jefferies on Nykaa

Buy TP raised to Rs 350

Continues to report strong growth across segments - beauty growth at >25% with  highest-ever EBITDA margin; fashion reported continued acceleration, with break-even signalling mgmt. has cracked code; own brands continue to go from strength to strength

Medium-term outlook is quite positive, although mgmt. sounded a note of caution in context of a tough macro.

HSBC on Nykaa

Hold, TP Rs 273

Robust GMV performance continues (BPC +27%, Fashion 31%); House of brands continues to scale (c20% of BPC)

EBITDA margin up c180bp y-o-y, similar to Q3FY26, yet the quantum of expansion is expected to moderate

Strong growth, execution but demanding valuation (67x FY28e EV/EBITDA)

CITI on Nykaa

Sell, TP Rs 225

EBITDA was 3% ahead of our estimates (8.4%; Citi: 8.2%) on in-line contribution profits (+1% vs Citi) and lower fixed opex.

Fashion achieved operating b/e (in-line with guidance).

Overall, Revenue/EBITDA/EBIT growth at 28%/67%/129% YoY.

BPC reported in-line gross margins and contribution profits (+29% YoY on +27% revenue growth).

Nykaa has delivered a notable uptick in margin expansion in FY26 (6% to 7.5%) – driven by lower losses in fashion, uptick in BPC gross margins and operating leverage

Bernstein on LIC 

Market Perform, TP Rs 900

Q4 saw healthy growth in top-line for LIC.

New sales grew +22%/+18% YoY in Q4/FY26 driven by strong non-par sales

Insurer saw continued margin uplift in FY26 aided by product mix shift (non-par) and favorable yield curve movements.

Management stated that margins could converge toward private peers level in medium-term, though acknowledged it will be a gradual process.

CITI on LIC

Buy,  TP Rs 1475

4Q operational performance was strong, led by ~690 bps YoY expansion in VNB margin (~600 bps over CitiE) aided by higher non-par mix (YoY) and favourable yield curve led benefits in the swiftly growing non-par book.

EV compressed 3% over Sep’25 (+2% over Mar’25), largely led by elevated negative investment variance (almost similar impact between debt and equity for FY2026).

Management highlighted that the drop in net gain on FV change, as of Mar ’26, has largely (around 80%) been recouped till mid-May’26 providing comfort on EV recoup.

Management highlighted concerted efforts to sustainably drive improvement in persistency, product innovation, increase productivity of exiting agents, agent base augmentation, and higher business through non-agency.

Valuation remains benign with FY2027E projected core EV (ex-MTM EV) at levels higher than current market value.

Visibility on promoter holding structure is a key overhang even as operational performance remains sound.

CITI on ITC

Sell, TP Rs 290

4Q performance is not directly comparable with prior periods given mid-quarter changes in cigarette taxation

Estimate ~7% volume decline, driving the 24% drop in cigarette net revenues, reflecting: 

1) strong January demand; 

2) trade inventory stocking ahead of price hikes; and 

3) demand slowdown thereafter following staggered price increases to offset higher taxes.

Reported cigarette EBIT growth of ~7% is also not comparable, as it appears supported by one-off inventory gains on pre-tax revision stock

Importantly, key structural uncertainties persist, particularly around price elasticity, demand sensitivity to successive price hikes, and potential downtrading to illicit products

@Stocksip

CLSA on ITC

O-P, TP Rs 394

Cigarettes Ebit grew 7.2% YoY in 4QFY26 and was 15% ahead of consensus. 

The quarter was marked by a significant increase in cigarette taxes, which likely led to channel filling and potential product mix changes due to pricing differentials, thereby impacting overall performance during the period. 

Cigarettes net revenue for the year grew 8.2% YoY. 

FMCG revenue other than cigarettes grew 15% YoY and was 2%/5% above our estimate/consensus.

@Concalls3

Hold TP Rs 38190

Consumer sentiment improving and a revival occurred in athleisure; Page took a calibrated price hike in Q4FY26

Increase revenue estimates but trim margins due to input cost inflation

Target Price ₹1300

Recommendation Neutral

Saw some softness in March due to decision making delays; will likely play out in Q1FY27 as well; H1 expected to be better than H2

Well set to participate in the six new Al driven value pools; partnerships and investments are in place

Jefferies on ITC

Target Price ₹350

Earlier Target ₹400

Recommendation Hold

Reported a strong Q4, driven by cigarette performance

Impact of sharp tax hike only partial, but 4Q also likely benefited from pipeline inventory around cut-off date

Real test is in Q1FY27 when impact of sharp tax hike will be visible

Price hikes are only partial while volumes & margins will be affected

Stock is likely to stay range-bound in near term

@Stocksip

Macquarie on Nykaa

Target Price ₹210

Recommendation Underperform

Q4: Healthy beauty margin, fashion breakeven

Q4 EBITDA was 5% ahead of estimate

Positives - strong gross margin in beauty, demand strength across segment, EBITDA break-even in fashion

Negatives - sub-15% growth for beauty ad income and moderating growth in own beauty brands

@Stockupdate9

Jefferies on Emami

Target Price ₹620

Earlier Target ₹650

Recommendation Buy

Another Quarter - Another Miss

Volatile trend in revenues continued

Revenue contraction had an obvious impact on EBITDA margins

EBITDA has remained near flat over the past five years

Unless growth picks up, the stock will remain out of favour

BUY is based solely on valuations
